coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of large language models and how they generate responses
Elementary knowledge of prompt structure and instruction following
Ability to wire a single model call into a script with supervision
Basic understanding of tool use, where a model calls an external function
Ability to define simple function schemas following examples
Elementary knowledge of reading and interpreting model outputs and logs
Basic understanding of what an agent is versus a single model call
Ability to use SDKs and playgrounds for experimentation
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of Python or a scripting language
Familiarity with JSON and structured data
Elementary understanding of API keys and environment variables
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can complete simple prompting tasks with detailed guidance
Needs 3-6 months to be productive

coreCompetencies: Ability to build a single-agent loop with tool calling and minimal supervision
Understanding of the reasoning and acting cycle where an agent plans and executes tools
Ability to design clear tool definitions with typed inputs and outputs
Practical knowledge of prompt templates and system instructions
Ability to handle tool errors and retries within an agent loop
Understanding of context windows and token limits
Ability to add basic conversational memory across turns
Knowledge of unit testing individual tools and prompts
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of vector databases and embeddings
Ability to use agent frameworks and their abstractions
Basic understanding of structured output and schema validation
indicators: Can build simple agents independently
Resolves medium complexity issues in agent behavior
Actively participates in code and prompt reviews

coreCompetencies: Mastery of single-agent design with robust tool orchestration
Ability to implement guardrails that constrain agent actions and outputs
Ability to design and run basic evaluations of agent task success
Deep knowledge of context management and prompt engineering techniques
Ability to implement short-term and long-term memory strategies
Understanding of retrieval to ground agent responses in external data
Ability to instrument agents with tracing to inspect each step
Knowledge of handling ambiguous inputs and clarifying questions
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of prompt injection risks and basic mitigations
Ability to implement human-in-the-loop approval steps
Basic understanding of multi-agent coordination
indicators: Completes complex agent features with minimal supervision
Identifies and resolves failures in agent reasoning loops
Actively contributes to technical design of agent systems

coreCompetencies: Ability to design multi-agent systems with clear roles and handoffs

Ability to build comprehensive evaluation suites with automated scoring

Ability to manage cost and latency across multi-step agent runs
Knowledge of failure recovery, fallback strategies and safe termination
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of fine-tuning and model selection for agent tasks
Ability to design tool ecosystems and reusable tool libraries
Understanding of sandboxing for agent code execution
indicators: Leads development of critical agent components
Effectively mentors junior engineers
Makes informed architectural decisions on agent design

Mastery of evaluation-driven development with offline and online metrics

Mastery of safety mechanisms including containment and permission models

indicators: Designs agent architectures for complete products
